perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, non- transferable, and (subject to Section 1.2 hereof) non-sublicensable license for the use of the Brand solely for the Permitted Activity.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee an exclusive, non- transferable, and (subject to Section 1.2 hereof) non-sublicensable license for the use of the Brand solely for the Permitted Activity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' creates a contradiction. Section 1.1 initially grants a non-exclusive license, meaning Licensor could allow other parties to use the Brand. Changing it to exclusive prevents Licensor from licensing the brand to other parties; this pertubation contradicts the implied right of the Licensor to continue using the brand themselves or to give it to another entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee acknowledges and agrees that, as between the parties, Licensor is the sole owner of all right, title and interest in and to the Brand. Licensee agrees not to do anything inconsistent with such ownership, including (i) filing to register any trademark or service mark containing the Brand or (ii) directly or indirectly challenging, contesting or otherwise Source: MORGAN STANLEY DIRECT LENDING FUND, 10-12G/A, 11/19/2019 disputing the validity, enforceability or Licensor's ownership of the Brand (and the associated goodwill), including without limitation, in any claim, allegation, action, demand, proceeding or suit ("Action") regarding enforcement of this Agreement or involving any third party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' significantly alters the rights granted under the license. The original text allowed the Licensor to grant licenses to other parties. The changed text prevents the Licensor from doing so. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Licensor is the sole owner of all right, title, and interest in the Brand, implying the right to use the brand themselves or grant it to other parties. By granting an exclusive license, the Licensor is prevented from exercising their ownership rights, creating a direct and significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues in perpetuity, unless termination occurs pursuant to Sections 4.2 through 4.4.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for a period of one year, unless termination occurs pursuant to Sections 4.2 through 4.4.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'in perpetuity' to 'for a period of one year' creates a direct contradiction. The agreement is now defined as having a fixed term of one year, which is contradicted by the survival clauses mentioned in section 4.5, and creating doubt about the survival clause and the long-term implications in other sections, that are now becoming ambigious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, (a) Licensee shall immediately, except as required by applicable Law, (i) cease all use of the Permitted Activity; and (b) the parties shall cooperate so as to best preserve the value of the Brand. Section 2, this Section 4.5, and Sections 6.2, 6.3, 7 and 9 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the agreement as continuing 'in perpetuity,' while the changed text limits the term to 'a period of one year.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict with Section 4.5, which specifies that certain sections (including 2, 4.5, 6.2, 6.3, 7, and 9) 'shall survive termination of this Agreement.' If the agreement terminates after one year, the survival clause becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, as it is unclear what obligations and rights would continue beyond the one-year term. This alteration introduces a strong ambiguity and contradiction, impacting the long-term implications of the agreement as described in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee may not assign, transfer, pledge, mortgage or otherwise encumber this Agreement or its right to use the Brand (or assume this Agreement in bankruptcy), in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Licensor in its sole discretion, except for an assignment outside of bankruptcy to a successor organization that is solely the result of a name change by Licensee.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may freely assign, transfer, pledge, mortgage or otherwise encumber this Agreement or its right to use the Brand (or assume this Agreement in bankruptcy), in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Licensor in its sole discretion, except for an assignment outside of bankruptcy to a successor organization that is solely the result of a name change by Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Licensee may not assign' to 'Licensee may freely assign' creates a contradiction, and ambiguity about the assignment process. This contradicts the the last sentece in the same section saying 'Any attempted action in violation of the foregoing shall be null and void ab initio and of no force or effect, and shall result in immediate termination of this Agreement.', as it enforces the assignment of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee may freely assign, transfer, pledge, mortgage or otherwise encumber this Agreement or its right to use the Brand (or assume this Agreement in bankruptcy), in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Licensor in its sole discretion, except for an assignment outside of bankruptcy to a successor organization that is solely the result of a name change by Licensee. For the avoidance of doubt, a merger, change of control, reorganization or sale of all or substantially all of the stock of Licensee shall be deemed an "assignment" requiring the above consent, regardless of whether Licensee is the surviving entity or whether such transaction constitutes an assignment under applicable law. Licensee acknowledges that its identity is a material condition that induced Licensor to enter into this Agreement. Any attempted action in violation of the foregoing shall be null and void ab initio and of no force or effect, and shall result in immediate termination of this Agreement. In the event of a permitted assignment hereunder, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Licensee may not assign', which is then changed to 'Licensee may freely assign'. This creates a direct contradiction with the subsequent sentences in the contradicted text that require Licensor's consent for assignment. The change fundamentally alters the assignment process and contradicts the condition that any assignment without consent is void and leads to termination.
